content.full_text:
CITY OF CARLSBAD
1200 CARLSBAD VILLAGE DRIVE
CARLSBAD, CALIFORNIA 92008-1989
(619) 434-2891
FAX NO. (619) 720-9461

VINCENT F. BIONDO, JR.
CITY ATTORNEY

RONALD R. BALL
ASSISTANT CITY ATTORNEY

KAREN J. HIRATA
DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY

January 18, 1991

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

RE: REQUEST FOR FORMAL ASSISTANCE PURSUANT TO COMMISSION
REGULATION 18329 AND GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 83114(b)

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

At the general election of November 1990, Ms. Margaret Stanton was
elected to the City Council of the City of Carlsbad and by virtue of
that position is also a member of the Carlsbad Redevelopment
Commission. She has authorized me to request formal written advice
on her behalf as to whether or not she may participate in projects,
as set forth below, now pending before the Redevelopment
Commission.

Commissioner Stanton does not own real property within the
redevelopment area. However, she is an officer and director of
Jazzercise, Inc. whose corporate offices are located there.
Jazzercise is a source of income to her exceeding $250 annually but
she has no ownership interest in the company which is wholly owned
by the Missett Family Trust. The office address is 2808 Roosevelt
Street near the intersection of Grand Avenue and Roosevelt Street
as shown on the Carlsbad Village Area boundary map, attached as
Exhibit A. The corporation operates approximately 4,500 franchises
worldwide. Its revenues from the franchises, a mall order and
retail store and its television operation are approximately $15
million annually. The corporation leased the approximately 26,000
square foot office in October 1990 and the lease will expire in
October 1995. The corporation operates a mail order and retail
store and a television production company at this location. It
also subleases a part of the offices to several of its local
franchisees for dance fitness classes. These franchisees pay to
Jazzercise 35% of the gross student fees collected per month as
rent and 20% of the gross student fees collected as a continuing
franchise fee.

Commissioner Stanton's mailing address is 4711 Sunburst Road,
Carlsbad, California 92008.

The City of Carlsbad has a redevelopment project area shown on
Exhibit A. There are six subareas with specific goals and desired
land uses. The corporate offices are located within subarea 5
which is planned as the major tourist and related commercial center
for the project area. The redevelopment plan was adopted in 1981
to create a pleasant, accessible environment for living, shopping, recreation, civic, cultural and service functions
by eliminating blight, restoring and constructing new, private
and public development to preserve and enhance the existing
character of the village.

Redevelopment projects now pending which will go to the
Redevelopment Commission for a vote in the near future are
described in detail as follows:

1.    A parking plan, including financing sources, to identify how
      parking needs for the village Redevelopment Area (Exhibit A)
      may be met for future projects. Preparation of the parking
      plan was funded by the redevelopment tax increment and
      benefits the entire project area. Implementation may involve
      the imposition of additional fees on property owners located
      within the area to finance the necessary improvements. It is
      also possible a surcharge will be added to the business
      license tax.

2.    The Commission is completing a major streetscape project
      funded by tax allocation bonds. A fifth phase of this project
      consisting of street improvements, decorative pavers, storm
      drains, traffic signals, street furniture, art work and street
      lighting is ready for approval. Phase II will cost
      approximately $1.2 million. It will be located on Carlsbad
      Village Drive (Elm Avenue) from Washington Street to Pio Pico.
      Traffic signals will be installed along Grand Avenue. The
      project is located from 500 - 1,000 feet from the Jazzerise
      corporate offices.

3.    The final design of a rapid rail transit center is being
      proposed and funded by the North County Transit District, a
      separate legal entity, and the County of San Diego. The
      Commission has signed a cooperative agreement with both
      agencies which requires our approval of the final design. The
      project will include a transit center structure, parking
      facilities and lighting. A bus transfer facility west of the
      railroad tracks will be constructed requiring relocation of an
      existing lumber business on State Street. The rapid rail
      transit center station site will be bounded on three sides by
      Carlsbad Village Drive (Elm Avenue), State Street and Grand
      Avenue and is approximately 1,000 feet from Jazzerise.

[The image appears to be a very faded or low-contrast document that is largely illegible. Most of the text is too faint to read accurately. There appear to be multiple paragraphs and sections, but the specific words cannot be reliably transcribed due to the poor image quality. Any attempt to transcribe the text would result in significant inaccuracy.]

4.     The Commission staff is preparing a master plan to determine
how the redevelopment area shall develop in the next ten to
twenty years. Elements of the plan will include but are not
limited to, the design, land use, historic preservation and
cultural arts, and financing of projects in the redevelopment
area. The plan will be funded from the redevelopment tax
increment. The master plan area will be coterminous with the
redevelopment area.

5.     The City Council has authorized the study and possible
formation of a second redevelopment project area generally
located along the Pacific Ocean and to the south of the
existing redevelopment area. The exact area of this proposed
second redevelopment area is shown on Exhibit B. The closest
point of the proposed second redevelopment area is 2,000 feet
from the Jazzercise corporate offices. The outer boundary is
6.5 miles away. It is also located approximately 2,000 feet
from Ms. Stanton's personal residence at 4711 Sunburst Road.
The next action will be appointment of the project area
committee and designation of the new project area.

6.     There is a Design Review Board within the Redevelopment Agency
which makes recommendations to the Agency on specific
projects. The Design Review Board has recommended approval of
a $3 million private development project which would allow the
construction of a three story, 28,817 square foot mixed use
office and retail building located at the southeast corner of
State Street and Laguna Drive approximately 2,000 feet from
Ms. Stanton's corporate offices. The project replaces an old
equipment rental business.

7.     An application has been submitted for a $150,000 3,100 square
foot retail project located at 2933 Roosevelt Street, 250 feet
from Ms. Stanton's corporate offices.

8.     An application is pending for a $25,000 project located at
2558 Roosevelt Street located 500 from the Jazzercise offices.
It consists of an intensification of the existing general
office use on the site to medical use which requires providing
parking on an enjoining lot by private lease.

9.     Within the redevelopment area there is an Army/Navy academy
located at the northwest corner of Cypress Avenue and Carlsbad
Boulevard. This non-profit organization is preparing for a
total renovation of its campus and has proposed a $20 million
construction project. The project is located 1,000 feet from
Ms. Stanton's corporate offices.

10.    A $2.5 million senior housing project is proposed at the
southwest corner of Carlsbad Boulevard and Beech Avenue. The
project will be a self-sufficient senior facility with meal
service. It is located from 1,000 - 2,000 feet from the
Jazzercise offices.

11.   A mixed-use retail and a condominium apartment complex with 20
affordable housing units is proposed for the southeast corner
of Carlsbad Boulevard and Beech Avenue 750 - 2,000 feet from
the Jazzercise offices.  The project will cost approximately
$15.5 million and may require financial assistance from the
Redevelopment Agency.  Two existing businesses and one
residence will be replaced.

Commissioner and City Council Member Stanton seeks your written
advice regarding these pending projects for her guidance as to
whether or not she may participate in them.  Neither Ms. Stanton
nor Jazzercise, Inc. has any financial interest in any of the above
described projects.  Ms. Stanton does not think any present a
conflict for her because of their minimal effect on the business
which obtains most if its revenue from the thousands of franchises
located outside Carlsbad.  She does not think any possible change
in the value of the office property will affect the value of
Jazzercise lease or have a material financial effect on the
corporation.

Although the list of projects is a lengthy one, it is anticipated
that your advice will assist her during her pendency before the
Agency or City Council.  There may be other projects that have not
yet been proposed or are too premature to list at this time and we
may seek further written advice regarding them in the future.
Please do not hesitate to contact me should you need more
information or details on any specific project listed above.

Very, truly yours,

[signature]
VINCENT T. BIONDO, JR.
City Attorney

EXHIBIT A

COMMISSIONER STANTON'S

ITEM 3

EXHIBIT "C" (AS AMENDED)     EXHIBIT B     City of Carlsbad

REDEVELOPMENT PROJECT II                    ALTERNATIVE "C"
